titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Solly Family genealogy collection 1990.022
titlestmt.author: Nicholas Pavlik
titlestmt.sponsor: This collection was originally processed and described as part of the project, "Uncovering the Secrets of Brooklyn's 19th Century Past: Creation to Consolidation," funded by the Council on Library and Information Resources, with additional support from The Gladys Krieble Delmas Foundation.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-04-19 22:39:14 +0000.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

physdesc.extent: in one folder and one oversize folder.

did.abstract: One manuscript volume by Nathaniel Neal Solly regarding various ancestors and family members and one oversize genealogical chart of the Solly family.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The two items in this collection are arranged in two folders by size.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date (if known); Solly Family genealogy collection, 1990.022, Box and Folder number; Brooklyn Public Library, Center for Brooklyn History.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Minimally processed to the collection level.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection includes one manuscript volume account by Nathaniel Neal Solly regarding various ancestors and family members. The volume gives a detailed account of the Solly family history and includes charts, seals, and a rendering of the family's coat of arms. Also included is one oversize genealogical chart of the Solly family. Although they primarily document the Solly family of Kent, England, the volume and chart were found in Brooklyn, N.Y. upon the building of St. Stephen's Lutheran Church School at Newkirk Avenue and 28th Street.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: The original accession also included four books that were transferred to the library's book collection.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The materials in this collection are in the public domain. While many items at the Center for Brooklyn History are unrestricted, we do not own reproduction rights to all materials. Be aware of the several kinds of rights that might apply: copyright, licensing and trademarks. The researcher assumes all responsibility for copyright questions.
